text: Tap out, Tommy. It's okay... I love you! I love you, Tommy!
context: Brendan chooses love and reconciliation over victory, breaking through his brother's armor in the cage.

text: We train. That's it. I don't wanna hear a word about anything but training, you understand?
context: Tommy's single-minded discipline and refusal to deal with anything but the fight in front of him.

text: I'm not a quitter. I don't tap.
context: The core of Tommy's identity: he will endure any pain rather than submit.

text: You never had any interest in underdogs. But I was your son.
context: Painful truth about a father's past failures and the sons left behind.

text: I think I liked you better when you were a drunk.
context: Raw anger at his father's attempt at redemption after years of absence and pain.

text: In the end, it's not about who hits harder. It's about who can take it and keep coming.
context: Echoing Rocky but in the brutal world of MMA — endurance of pain as the true measure.

text: I'm gonna break your arm. And then I'm gonna break the other one. And then I'm gonna break your legs.
context: The terrifying, focused intensity Tommy brings — fighting as war, not sport.
The line that finally allows Tommy to let go and find peace after years of carrying pain.
